_comment: Shared parity vectors for the piecewise-linear floor(+1e-9 epsilon) score->cap algorithm that is duplicated in Go (services/content-verify/internal/service/curve.go Curve.Cap) and Python (services/stealth-scraper/app/scaling.py pool_target_for). The two implementations cannot share code across the Go/Python boundary, so this fixture pins their shared INTERPOLATION+FLOOR+EPSILON core against one canonical vector set — a future edit to either that diverges is caught by both test suites. Cases stay within [1, max_pool] on purpose: the service-specific CLAMPS differ (Go allows 0; Python floors at 1) and are each tested separately. The 0.55 -> 3 case is the float-repr boundary the epsilon exists for (6 - 4*0.75 evaluates to 2.9999999999999987, not 3.0).
